news.headline: Dollar General beats Q4 sales expectations, with 4.5% growth to $10.304 billion. Plans to close 96 Dollar General stores and 45 pOpshelf stores. CEO mentions improved execution.
news.summary: Dollar General Corporation (NYSE:DG) exceeded expectations with its fourth-quarter sales, reporting a 4.5% year-on-year increase to $10.304 billion, surpassing the anticipated $10.264 billion. However, the EPS of $0.87 fell short of the projected $1.51. CEO Todd Vasos expressed satisfaction with the fourth-quarter business performance, citing enhanced execution and strong top-line outcomes. During the quarter, the company conducted a store portfolio evaluation, leading to the decision to shut down 96 Dollar General stores and 45 pOpshelf stores. Additionally, six pOpshelf stores will be converted to Dollar General stores.

news.headline: U.S. Gold Corp. will attend the 37th Annual ROTH Conference for Growth Companies in California. Nasdaq: USAU.
news.summary: U.S. Gold Corp., trading on the Nasdaq as USAU, will participate in the 37th Annual ROTH Conference for Growth Companies in Cheyenne, Wyoming on March 14, 2025.

news.headline: Trump administration plans to increase Bitcoin reserves; aims for substantial accumulation in cost-neutral strategy. Key figures like Michael Saylor involved.
news.summary: A high-ranking representative from the White House revealed this week to leaders in the cryptocurrency industry that the Trump administration is looking to increase its Bitcoin holdings as part of a broader financial plan. Bo Hines, who leads the Presidential Working Group on Digital Assets, disclosed during a private meeting organized by the Bitcoin Policy Institute that the government aims to acquire a significant amount of Bitcoin. The meeting, which took place after a public event titled "Bitcoin for America," was attended by prominent figures in the sector, including Michael Saylor from Strategy (NASDAQ: MSTR), Fred Thiel from Marathon Digital (NASDAQ: MARA), Nathan McCauley from Anchorage Digital, and David Bailey from Bitcoin Magazine.

news.headline: Lgmusicpub adds hit song "Phenomenal" with Snoop Dogg in publishing deal; Brian Elliott Smith involved.
news.summary: Atlanta, March 14, 2025 - Today, Lgmusicpub Publishing Agency Int'l LLC shared the exciting news of their recent signing of the hit song "Phenomenal" produced by Brian Elliott Smith, the CEO of 804 Music / Bard Up Entertainment. "Phenomenal" is set to make waves in the music industry with its unique sound and fresh approach, promising to captivate audiences worldwide.

news.headline: Rep. Donalds proposes bill to secure Trump's Bitcoin reserve, preventing dismantling via executive action. At least 23 states support.
news.summary: The article discusses a new bill to solidify President Trump's executive order establishing a US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve, utilizing Bitcoin seized in criminal cases to create a national reserve. The legislation introduced by US Representative Donalds aims to make the reserve permanent to prevent future administrations from dismantling it. 23 US states have also proposed similar legislation. The bill requires 60 Senate votes and a House majority to pass. This move signifies the increasing acceptance and integration of cryptocurrency into US financial policy. The bill could provide clarity for Bitcoin acquisition strategies and regulatory approaches in the future, although challenges such as funding and custody solutions need to be addressed. The bill may promote a more unified regulatory approach benefiting businesses and investors in the crypto space.

news.headline: SAIC to report Q4 results with estimated earnings of $2.09/share and revenue of $1.81 billion. Offers 1.42% dividend yield.
news.summary: On Monday, March 17, Science Applications International Corporation (NASDAQ: SAIC) is set to announce its Q4 financial results. Analysts predict earnings to be $2.09 per share, higher than the $1.43 per share reported last year. The company anticipates revenue of $1.81 billion for the quarter compared to $1.74 billion a year ago. SAIC's current annual dividend yield stands at 1.42%, with a quarterly payout of 37 cents per share ($1.48 annually). Investors are considering leveraging this dividend yield for potential gains amidst the recent interest in SAIC. For more information, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Rumble sets Q4 & FY 2024 earnings release & conference call. Also, investor conference participation scheduled.
news.summary: The latest report reveals concerning information about the impact of the recent natural disaster in the region. The disaster has left over 10,000 people displaced from their homes and in need of urgent assistance. The death toll has risen to 50, with many more injured and missing. The government has allocated $5 million in emergency funds for rescue and relief efforts. Rescue teams are working tirelessly to search for survivors and provide necessary aid. The situation remains critical, with infrastructure severely damaged and access to basic necessities limited. The community is urged to come together and support those affected by this tragedy.

news.headline: Surf Bio appoints Doug Hecker as Head of Technical Operations, bringing 30+ years of CMC expertise to advance biologic delivery.
news.summary: With over 30 years of experience in CMC (Chemistry, Manufacturing, and Controls), Doug Hecker has joined Surf Bio in Palo Alto, California. Surf Bio focuses on improving the delivery of antibodies and biologics. Doug Hecker's appointment as Head of the company comes as a strategic move to leverage his expertise in this field. The company aims to enhance the delivery of high-concentration biologics under his leadership. This announcement was made on March 14, 2025.

news.headline: Texas Parasport rebrands flagship event to Texas Parasport Games. Registration open for 2025 event. Athletes of all levels welcome to participate.
news.summary: Registration is now available for the 2025 event in San Antonio, scheduled for March 14. Texas Parasport, a prominent adaptive sports group supporting athletes with physical disabilities, has renamed its primary event. Athletes of all skill levels are encouraged to participate.

news.headline: Panama authorizes export of 120,000 tons of copper concentrate from Cobre Panama mine, seeking resolution and reimbursement from First Quantum Minerals.
news.summary: The President of Panama, Jose Raul Mulino, has allowed for the export of 120,000 tons of copper concentrate that have been stored at the Cobre Panama mine since its closure in December 2023. Mulino stated that this action was necessary as the material was being wasted and a significant amount of money had been invested in it by Panama. He emphasized the importance of handling the mine issue responsibly, considering national interests. The mine is operated by First Quantum Minerals, a Canadian company, which will be required to reimburse Panama for the material once it is processed abroad. Former President Laurentino Cortizo had initially shut down the Cobre Panama mine.

news.headline: Hudson Global reported 2024 results: Q4 revenue $33.6M, FY revenue $140.1M. Q4 net loss $0.6M, FY net loss $4.8M. Adjusted EBITDA increased to $0.9M in Q4 vs $0.1M in 2023. Q4 Asia Pacific revenue $20M, EMEA revenue $6.3M. Cash and cash equivalents $17.7M. Hudson invested $3.4M in sales, marketing, and technology in 2024. Hudson purchased 154,084 shares for $2.5M in 2024 under the share repurchase program. NOL Carryforward of $240M as of Dec 31, 2024.
news.summary: Hudson Global, Inc. reported its financial results for Q4 and full year 2024. In Q4, revenue was $33.6 million (1.1% decrease from Q4 2023), with adjusted net revenue at $17.6 million (6.4% increase from Q4 2023). Net loss in Q4 was $0.6 million, an adjusted EBITDA of $0.9 million. For the full year, revenue was $140.1 million (13.2% decrease from 2023), adjusted net revenue was $70.2 million, net loss was $4.8 million, and adjusted EBITDA was $0.9 million. The company invested $3.4 million in sales, marketing, and technology. Americas showed growth, with revenue at $27.9 million, adjusted net revenue at $25.1 million, and EBITDA at $0.3 million for the full year 2024. Asia Pacific revenue was $86.7 million, adjusted net revenue was $29.4 million, and EBITDA was $0.5 million for 2024. EMEA revenue was $25.5 million, adjusted net revenue was $15.6 million, and EBITDA was $0.3 million for 2024. The Company had $17.7 million in cash at the end of Q4 2024 and had used $2.8 million in cash flow from operations for the full year 2024. They also repurchased $2.5 million worth of shares and had $2.1 million remaining under the $5 million share repurchase program. Hudson Global had $240 million in usable net operating losses.

news.headline: Global structural heart devices market valued at US$14.93B in 2023, set to grow at a CAGR of 9.5% to reach US$25.69B by 2029. Dominant segment is transcatheter heart valves; North America leads market share.
news.summary: The global structural heart devices market was valued at US$14.93 billion in 2023 and is projected to grow at a CAGR of 9.5%, reaching US$16.31 billion in 2024 and a remarkable US$25.69 billion by 2029. The growth is attributed to the rise in valvular and congenital heart diseases, advancements in minimally invasive technologies like TAVR and TMVR, and increasing adoption due to an aging population and improved healthcare infrastructure worldwide. The market is segmented into Heart Valve Devices, Occluder, Closure systems, Delivery systems, and Annuloplasty rings. The Transcatheter Heart Valve segment is particularly dominant due to factors like increasing prevalence of valvular heart diseases, aging population, technological innovations, and preference for minimally invasive procedures. North America leads in market share, driven by a sophisticated healthcare infrastructure. Key players in the market are mentioned in the Benzinga.com article.

news.headline: Palisade Bio starts dosing first UC patient in Phase 1a/b study of PALI-2108. Positive data from 5 SAD and 3 MAD cohorts show safety and tolerability.
news.summary: Initial findings from the five single ascending dose (SAD) groups and the initial three complete multiple ascending dose (MAD) groups indicate positive results, confirming the safety and tolerability of PALI-2108.
